what_it_does: Defines an HTML page that mounts a rendered decision-card slide for the hard-coded decision id "cluster-identity" by loading layout and decision JSON, creating a decision archetype render, wrapping it in an explained slide view, and appending it to #mount.
observations: The page imports shared typography from Google Fonts and shared CSS from /surface/styles/piece.css and /surface/styles/phone.css.
Inline CSS resets html/body spacing, sets the body background to var(--warm), and makes #mount a full-height inline-size container.
The decision record is selected by the constant ID "cluster-identity".
The rendered identity merges the decision record with a decision://global/cluster-identity address and a forced "pending" state.
The final rendered view gets the class "dv-c" before being appended to #mount.
standouts: A single hard-coded ID drives a generative render path.
The file composes archetype rendering with a slide explanation wrapper.
State is explicitly resolved as "pending" in both identity data and resolver behavior.
conventions: Uses a minimal HTML shell with a dedicated #mount target.
Uses an async IIFE for page initialization.
Relies on global runtime objects loaded by script tags before the inline script runs.
Keeps decision-specific variation in the ID rather than duplicating markup.
Uses fetch-based JSON inputs for layout and content data.
concerns: No local handling for failed fetches or invalid JSON.
No guard if the decision record or decision-card archetype is missing.
The resolver silently returns an empty string for unrecognized names.
notes: This is a small generated decision-view page whose local role is to render the cluster-identity decision through the shared DNA runtime.
questions: Should the page show a fallback if "cluster-identity" is not found?
Why is the decision state fixed to "pending" here?
Which resolver names besides "explanation_source" and "state" may be requested by the runtime?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the comment matches the implementation: it builds a composed decision view containing a rendered decision interface and explanatory slide wrapper.
intention_signals: It seems intended to be a file-local instance of a reusable decision-card slide system, where the concrete page is produced by selecting one decision id and passing it through shared layout, content, and rendering primitives.
novelty: The page is mostly declarative shell code, with the unusual part being the address-bearing archetype render wrapped by a separate explained-slide render.
contribution: Contributes a concrete decision-card page instance for "cluster-identity", including the exact mount shell, runtime asset wiring, decision address construction, pending-state setup, and explained-slide composition needed to preserve this substrate in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: HTML page for counterpart-design that renders the "cluster-identity" decision-card as a composed explained slide using shared DNA layout, content, and runtime scripts.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to instantiate one reusable decision-card renderer for the cluster-identity decision while keeping the interface, explanation, address, and visual device co-visible in a generated slide view.
